'''''The Far Orbit Project''''' was written by [[Timothy S. O'Brien]] for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'', published by [[West End Games]] in [[1998]]. It is a campaign book detailing adventures within a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Privateer/Legends|privateering]] operation using the guidelines seen in the previous publication, ''[[Pirates & Privateers]]''.

One of the book's main features is a partial map of an [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate]], detailing 14 decks in 12 pages.

''The Far Orbit Project'' was the last original book published by West End Games, only preceding the re-edition of ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'' collection in a boxed set.

==Publisher's summary==
''Join the fearless crew of the ''Far Orbit'', the first Rebel privateer ship. The Far Orbit's mission is simple: stage a daring series of strikes on the Core Worlds and wreak havoc on the forces of the Empire. The Far Orbit Project is the first supplement designed for use with the material presented in ''Pirates & Privateers''.''

==Opening crawl==
{{OpeningCrawl
|name=
|text=It is a dangerous time for the Rebel Alliance. Following the destruction of the Death Star battle station at the Battle of Yavin, the Emperor has ordered the annihilation of those who would oppose his rule. Vast fleets of Imperial ships have been dispatched to the far corners of the galaxy to root out and destroy the Rebels wherever they can be found.

In this desperate hour, the Rebel Alliance needs supplies, equipment and weapons to pursue its mission of liberating the galaxy from the forces of tyranny. To further this goal, Mon Mothma and other Alliance leaders have issues Letters of Marque and Reprisal to a select group of private raiding ships: Rebel Privateers.

One of the first privateers to take up the dangerous mission of harassing Imperial ships is the Far Orbit, a captured Nebulon-B escort frigate crewed by a motley collection of Imperial mutineers and former pirates.

The captain of the Far Orbit, Dhas Vedij, was once a capable and respected officer in the Imperial Navy. Now, he has volunteered to spearhead a series of daring strikes into the Ringali Shell, deep in the heart of Palpatine's domain. But, unbeknownst to Vedij, an old enemy awaits him in the Core Worlds....
}}

==Contents==
*Introduction
*Chapter One: The ''Far Orbit''
*Chapter Two: The [[Ringali Shell]]
*Chapter Three: Enemies of the ''Far Orbit''
*Chapter Four: The Fringe
*Chapter Five: Gamemaster's section
*Adventure One: ''[[Preparing for War]]''
*Adventure Two: ''[[The Hijacking of Shipment 1037]]''
*Adventure Three: ''[[The Capture of Coh Veshiv]]''
*Adventure Four: ''[[The Rival]]''
*Adventure Five: [[The Trap (adventure scenario)|''The Trap'']]
*Adventure Six: ''[[Raid on Brentaal]]''
*Adventure Seven: ''[[Hunter/Hunted]]''
*Adventure Eight: ''[[The Grand Prize]]''
*Epilogue
*Apendix A
